declarations.description:
A popover menu component that displays a list of menu items in a floating container.
It's designed to work in conjunction with `mdc-menubar` and `mdc-menuitem` to create
accessible, nested menu structures with the following features:
- Appears adjacent to the triggering menu item
- Supports keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Home, End)
- Manages focus trapping when open
- Closes on Escape key or outside click
- Supports both mouse and keyboard interactions
- Automatically handles ARIA attributes for accessibility

The component extends `mdc-popover` and adds menu-specific behaviors and styling.
When nested within another `mdc-menupopover`, it automatically adjusts its behavior
to work as a submenu (right-aligned, shows on hover).

The orientation of the menu popover is always set to `vertical`.

Submenu opens when:
- Clicked on a menu item with a submenu
- Enter or Space key pressed on a menu item with a submenu

Menu closes completely (with all sub menus) when:
- A menu item is clicked that does not have a submenu
- Enter key pressed on a menu item (not a submenu trigger), menu item radio or menu item checkbox
- Click outside the menu popover (on the backdrop)

Close submenus when:
- Esc key pressed, only the current submenu closed
- Arrow Left key pressed, only the current submenu closed
- Open another submenu with Click, Enter or Space key,
  closes recursively all submenus until the selected item's submenu

Menu does not close when:
- Space key pressed on a menu item radio or menu item checkbox
declarations.name: MenuPopover

slots.description: Default slot for the menu popover content
Contains the menu items to be displayed in the popover
slots.name: default

members.name: placement
members.description:
The placement of the popover.
- **top**
- **top-start**
- **top-end**
- **bottom**
- **bottom-start**
- **bottom-end**
- **left**
- **left-start**
- **left-end**
- **right**
- **right-start**
- **right-end**

members.name: isOpenUpdated
members.description:
Handles the popover visibility change and position the popover.
Handles the exit event to close the popover.

members.name: resetTabIndexes

parameters.name: currentIndex
parameters.description: The index of the currently focused menu item.

members.description: Reset all tabindex to -1 and set the tabindex of the current menu item to 0

members.name: getCurrentIndex

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target element that triggered the event.

members.description: Retrieves the current index of the menu item that triggered the event.

members.name: resetTabIndexAndSetFocus

parameters.name: newIndex
parameters.description: The index of the new menu item to focus.

parameters.name: oldIndex
parameters.description: The index of the currently focused menu item.

members.description: Resets the tabindex of the currently focused menu item and sets focus to a new menu item.

members.name: closeAllMenuPopovers
members.description:
Closes all menu popovers in the stack.
This method is used to ensure that when a menu item is clicked,
all other open popovers are closed, maintaining a clean user interface.
It iterates through the `popoverStack` and hides each popover until the stack is empty.

members.name: onOutsidePopoverClick
members.description:
Handles outside click events to close the popover.
This method checks if the click occurred outside the popover and its trigger element.
If so, it closes the popover by calling `closeAllMenuPopovers`.
It also checks if the click was on the backdrop element (if present) to close the popover.

parameters.description: The mouse event that triggered the outside click.
parameters.name: event

members.name: togglePopoverVisible
members.description:
Toggles the visibility of the popover.
This method checks if the trigger element has the `soft-disabled` attribute.
If it does, the popover will not be toggled.
If the popover is currently visible, it hides the popover; otherwise, it shows the popover.

members.name: closeOtherSubMenusOnSameLevel

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target menu item that was clicked.

members.description:
Closes all other submenus on the same level as the target menu item.
This method is used to ensure that only one submenu is open at a time.
It finds all other menu items with submenus and closes their submenus.

members.name: fireMenuItemAction

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target menu item that was clicked or activated.

members.description:
Fires the 'action' event on the target menu item.
This method is used to trigger the action associated with a menu item when it is clicked or activated.
It checks if the target element matches the `MENUITEM_TAGNAME` and dispatches an 'action' event.

members.name: handleMouseClick

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: The mouse event that triggered the click.

members.description:
Handles mouse click events on the menu items.
This method checks if the clicked element is a valid menu item and not a submenu trigger.
If it is, it closes all other menu popovers to ensure only one menu is open at a time.

members.name: resolveDirectionKey

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The key pressed by the user.

parameters.name: isRtl
parameters.description: A boolean indicating if the layout is right-to-left (RTL).

members.description:
Resolves the key pressed by the user based on the direction of the layout.
This method is used to handle keyboard navigation in a right-to-left (RTL) layout.
It checks if the layout is RTL and adjusts the arrow keys accordingly.
For example, in RTL, the left arrow key behaves like the right arrow key and vice versa.

members.name: handleKeyDown
members.description:
Handles keydown events for keyboard navigation within the menu popover.
This method allows users to navigate through the menu items using the keyboard.
It supports Home, End, Arrow Up, Arrow Down, Arrow Left, Arrow Right, and Escape keys.

Also, it handles Enter key to close the menu popover

parameters.description: The keyboard event that triggered the keydown action.
parameters.name: event

members.name: handleKeyUp
members.description:
Handles keyup events for keyboard navigation within the menu popover.

Some keys must be handled with keyup event to prevent default action.

Space key closes the menu when the user presses it on a menu item,
but the same key will trigger a click on the menu opener button.
The button uses the keyup event so we have to handle it here as well
to prevent the meu opener action which would re-open the menu.

parameters.description: The keyboard event that triggered the keydown action.
parameters.name: event

members.name: zIndex
members.description:
IMPLEMENT THIS IN YOUR COMPONENT.

The z-index of the component where the backdrop will be attached to.
The backdrop itself will have a z-index of `zIndex - 1`.

members.name: preventScroll
members.description:
Prevent outside scrolling when element is shown.

IMPLEMENT THIS IN YOUR COMPONENT.

members.name: focusTrap
members.description:
Determines whether the focus trap is enabled.
If true, focus will be restricted to the content within this component.

IMPLEMENT THIS IN YOUR COMPONENT.

members.name: shouldFocusTrapWrap
members.description:
Determines whether focus should wrap around when reaching the first or last focusable element.
If true, focus will cycle from end to start and vice versa.

This only applies when `focusTrap` is true.

members.name: activateFocusTrap
members.description: Activate the focus trap

members.name: deactivateFocusTrap
members.description: Deactivate the focus trap.

members.name: hasNoClientRects
members.description: Checks if the element has no client rectangles (not visible in the viewport).

members.name: hasZeroDimensions
members.description: Checks if the element has zero dimensions (width and height are both 0).

members.name: isNotVisible
members.description: Determines if the element is not visible in the DOM.

members.name: hasHiddenStyle
members.description: Checks if the element has inline styles that make it hidden.

members.name: hasComputedHidden
members.description: Checks if the element is hidden by a computed style.

members.name: isHidden
members.description: Checks if the element is hidden from the user.

members.name: isDisabled
members.description: Checks if the element is disabled.

members.name: isNotTabbable
members.description: Checks if the element is not tabbable.

members.name: isInteractiveElement
members.description: Checks if the element is interactive.

members.name: isFocusable
members.description: Checks if the element is focusable.

members.name: findFocusable

parameters.name: root
parameters.description: The root element to search for focusable elements.

members.description:
Recursively finds all focusable elements within the given root and its descendants.

Make sure this is performant, as it will be called multiple times.

members.name: setFocusableElements
members.description: Updates the list of focusable elements within the component's shadow root.

members.name: setInitialFocus

parameters.name: elementIndexToReceiveFocus
parameters.description: The index of the preferable element to focus.

members.description: Sets the initial focus within the container.

members.name: calculateNextIndex

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: The step to calculate the next index.

members.description: Calculates the next index for the focus trap.

members.name: getDeepActiveElement
members.description: Returns the deepest active element in the shadow DOM.

members.name: findElement
members.description: Finds the index of the active element within the focusable elements.

members.name: isEqualFocusNode
members.description: Checks if the active element is equal to the given element.

members.name: trapFocus

parameters.description:
The direction of the focus trap.
If true, the focus will be trapped in the previous element.
parameters.name: direction

members.description: Traps focus within the container.

members.name: handleTabKeydown
members.description: Traps focus within the container.

members.name: id
members.description: The unique ID of the popover.

members.name: triggerID
members.description:
The ID of the element that triggers the popover.
This attribute is required for the popover to work.

members.name: trigger
members.description:
Determines the events that cause the Popover to show.
Multiple event names should be separated by spaces.
For example to allow both click and hover, use 'click mouseenter' as the trigger.
- **click**
- **mouseenter**
- **focusin**
- **manual**

members.name: color
members.description:
Color of the popover
- **tonal**
- **contrast**

members.name: visible
members.description: The visibility of the popover.

members.name: offset
members.description: The offset of the popover.

members.name: boundary
members.description:
This describes the clipping element(s) or area that overflow will be checked relative to.
The default is 'clippingAncestors', which are the overflow ancestors which will cause the
element to be clipped.

Possible values:
 - 'clippingAncestors'
 - any css selector

members.name: boundaryRoot
members.description:
This describes the root boundary that the element will be checked for overflow relative to.
The default is 'viewport', which is the area of the page the user can see on the screen.

The other string option is 'document', which is the entire page outside the viewport.

members.name: boundaryPadding
members.description:
Virtual padding around the boundary to check for overflow.
So the popover will not be placed on top of the edge of the boundary.

Default works well for most cases, but you can set this to customise it when necessary.

members.name: showArrow
members.description: The arrow visibility of the popover.

members.name: closeButton
members.description: The close button visibility of the popover.

members.name: interactive
members.description:
Determines whether the popover is interactive.
Make sure to set focusTrap to true to keep the focus inside the popover in case necessary.
Setting interactive to true will not automatically set focusTrap!

members.name: delay
members.description: The delay of the show/hide popover.

members.name: hideOnEscape
members.description: Hide popover on escape key press.

members.name: propagateEventOnEscape
members.description:
Propagates the event, when the escape key is pressed (only when pressed inside the popover)
If true, the escape key press close the popover and will propagate the keydown event.
If false, the escape key press will close the popover but will not propagate the keydown event.
(set to false to prevent the event from bubbling up to the document).

This only works when `hideOnEscape` is true.

members.name: hideOnBlur
members.description: Hide popover on blur.

members.name: hideOnOutsideClick
members.description: Hide on outside click of the popover.

members.name: focusBackToTrigger
members.description: The focus back to trigger after the popover hide.

members.name: backdrop
members.description:
Determines whether the popover with backdrop.
Other than popover and trigger element, the rest of the screen will be covered with a backdrop.

members.name: backdropAppendTo
members.description: Element ID the backdrop will be a append to (if `backdrop` is true).

members.name: isBackdropInvisible
members.description:
Set this property to false to make the backdrop a dimmed visible backdrop (if `backdrop` is true).
This is useful for cases where the popovers backdrop should get dimmed.
members.default: true - default is to have a invisible backdrop

members.name: flip
members.description: Changes the placement of popover to keep it in view when scrolling.

members.name: size
members.description: Changes the size of popover to keep it in view when scrolling.

members.name: appendTo
members.description: Element ID that the popover append to.

members.name: closeButtonAriaLabel
members.description: aria-label attribute to be set for close button accessibility.

members.name: strategy
members.description:
The strategy of the popover.
This determines how the popover is positioned in the DOM.
- **absolute**: The popover is positioned absolutely relative to the nearest positioned ancestor.
- **fixed**: The popover is positioned fixed relative to the viewport.

Default as `absolute` is recommended for most cases.
In cases where the popover gets clipped by a scrollable container,
you can set this to `fixed` to avoid clipping.

members.name: role
members.description: Role of the popover

members.name: ariaLabelledby
members.description:
aria-labelledby for an interactive popover only, defaults to the trigger component id.
Used in nested cases where the triggerComponent isn't the actual button.

members.name: ariaDescribedby
members.description: aria-describedby of the popover.

members.name: disableAriaExpanded
members.description:
Disable setting the aria-expanded attribute on trigger element.
Make sure to set this to false when the popover is interactive.

members.name: disableAriaHasPopup
members.description:
Disable setting the aria-haspopup attribute on trigger element.
Make sure to set this to true when the popover is extended and its role
is not 'dialog' or 'alertdialog' i.e. listbox, menu, etc.

members.name: keepConnectedTooltipClosed
members.description:
If a tooltip is connected to the same trigger element,
this property will keep the connected tooltip closed if this popover is open.
This is useful when you want to show a popover with a tooltip
but you don't want the tooltip to be shown at the same time.

members.name: setupTriggerListeners
members.description:
Sets up the trigger related event listeners, based on the trigger type.
Includes fallback for mouseenter trigger to also handle focusin for non-interactive popovers.

members.name: removeTriggerListeners
members.description: Removes the trigger related event listeners.

members.name: removeAllListeners
members.description: Removes all event listeners related to the popover.

members.name: onEscapeKeydown
members.description:
Handles the escape keydown event to close the popover.

This method is attached to the document.

members.name: onPopoverFocusOut
members.description: Handles the popover focus out event.

members.name: handleMouseEnter
members.description:
Handles mouse enter event on the trigger element.
This method sets the `isHovered` flag to true and shows the popover

members.name: handleMouseLeave
members.description:
Handles mouse leave event on the trigger element.
This method sets the `isHovered` flag to false and starts the close delay
timer to hide the popover.

members.name: handleFocusOut
members.description:
Handles focus out event on the trigger element.
This method checks if the popover is not hovered and hides the popover.
If the popover is hovered, it will not hide the popover.

members.name: handleFocusIn
members.description:
Handles focus in event on the trigger element.
This method checks if the trigger element has visible focus or is being hovered.

members.name: startCloseDelay
members.description:
Starts the close delay timer.
If the popover is not interactive, it will close the popover after the delay.

members.name: cancelCloseDelay
members.description: Cancels the close delay timer.

members.name: show
members.description: Shows the popover.

members.name: hide
members.description: Hides the popover.

members.name: positionPopover
members.description:
Positions the popover based on the trigger element.
It also handles the flip, size and arrow placement.
It uses the floating-ui/dom library to calculate the position.

members.name: findClosestPopover
members.description:
Finds the closest popover to the passed element in the DOM tree.

Useful when need to find the parent popover in a nested popover scenario.

parameters.description: The element to start searching from.
parameters.name: element

events.description: (React: onChange) This event is dispatched when a `menuitemcheckbox`, or `menuitemradio` changes.
events.name: change

events.description: (React: onAction) This event is dispatched when a menuItem selected and the menu closes.
events.name: action

events.description: (React: onShown) This event is dispatched when the popover is shown
events.name: shown

events.description: (React: onHidden) This event is dispatched when the popover is hidden
events.name: hidden

events.name: created

events.description: (React: onDestroyed) This event is dispatched when the popover is destroyed (removed from the DOM)
events.name: destroyed

declarations.jsDoc:
/**
 * A popover menu component that displays a list of menu items in a floating container.
 * It's designed to work in conjunction with `mdc-menubar` and `mdc-menuitem` to create
 * accessible, nested menu structures with the following features:
 * - Appears adjacent to the triggering menu item
 * - Supports keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Home, End)
 * - Manages focus trapping when open
 * - Closes on Escape key or outside click
 * - Supports both mouse and keyboard interactions
 * - Automatically handles ARIA attributes for accessibility
 *
 * The component extends `mdc-popover` and adds menu-specific behaviors and styling.
 * When nested within another `mdc-menupopover`, it automatically adjusts its behavior
 * to work as a submenu (right-aligned, shows on hover).
 *
 * The orientation of the menu popover is always set to `vertical`.
 *
 * Submenu opens when:
 * - Clicked on a menu item with a submenu
 * - Enter or Space key pressed on a menu item with a submenu
 *
 * Menu closes completely (with all sub menus) when:
 * - A menu item is clicked that does not have a submenu
 * - Enter key pressed on a menu item (not a submenu trigger), menu item radio or menu item checkbox
 * - Click outside the menu popover (on the backdrop)
 *
 * Close submenus when:
 * - Esc key pressed, only the current submenu closed
 * - Arrow Left key pressed, only the current submenu closed
 * - Open another submenu with Click, Enter or Space key,
 *   closes recursively all submenus until the selected item's submenu
 *
 * Menu does not close when:
 * - Space key pressed on a menu item radio or menu item checkbox
 *
 * @tagname mdc-menupopover
 *
 * @slot - Default slot for the menu popover content
 *
 * @event change - (React: onChange) This event is dispatched when a `menuitemcheckbox`, or `menuitemradio` changes.
 * @event action - (React: onAction) This event is dispatched when a menuItem selected and the menu closes.
 *
 * @slot default - Contains the menu items to be displayed in the popover
 */

declarations.description:
`mdc-menusection` is a container element used to group a set of menu items.

This component supports a mix of `menuitem`, `menuitemcheckbox`, and `menuitemradio` components.

- If multiple `menuitemradio` components are slotted into the section, the group enforces a single-selection rule:
  only one radio item can be selected at a time.
- If `menuitemcheckbox` components are included, their checked state can be toggled independently.
declarations.name: MenuSection

slots.description: Default slot for inserting `menuitem`, `menuitemcheckbox`, or `menuitemradio`

members.name: ariaLabel
members.description:
The aria-label for the section.
This is used for accessibility purposes to describe the section.
If not provided, it defaults to the `headerText`.

members.name: headerText
members.description:
The primary headerText of the list item.
This appears on the leading side of the list item.

members.name: prefixIcon
members.description:
Name of the icon rendered before the text

If not provided, no icon will be rendered and text will be aligned to the start.

members.name: showDivider
members.description:
Whether to show a divider below the section header.
This is useful for visually separating sections in the menu.

members.name: dividerVariant
members.description:
The variant of the divider.
Can be set to 'solid' or 'gradient'.

Keep 'solid' if used in MenuPopovers, as it is the default style.

events.description: (React: onChange) This event is dispatched when a `menuitemcheckbox`, or `menuitemradio` changes.
events.name: change

events.description: (React: onAction) This event is dispatched when a `menuitem` selected.
events.name: action

attributes.description:
The aria-label for the section.
This is used for accessibility purposes to describe the section.
If not provided, it defaults to the `headerText`.

The primary headerText of the list item.
This appears on the leading side of the list item.

attributes.name: prefix-icon
attributes.description:
Name of the icon rendered before the text

If not provided, no icon will be rendered and text will be aligned to the start.

This is useful for visually separating sections in the menu.

attributes.name: divider-variant

The variant of the divider.
Can be set to 'solid' or 'gradient'.

Keep 'solid' if used in MenuPopovers, as it is the default style.

declarations.jsDoc:
/**
 * `mdc-menusection` is a container element used to group a set of menu items.
 *
 * This component supports a mix of `menuitem`, `menuitemcheckbox`, and `menuitemradio` components.
 *
 * - If multiple `menuitemradio` components are slotted into the section, the group enforces a single-selection rule:
 *   only one radio item can be selected at a time.
 * - If `menuitemcheckbox` components are included, their checked state can be toggled independently.
 *
 * @tagname mdc-menusection
 *
 * @slot - Default slot for inserting `menuitem`, `menuitemcheckbox`, or `menuitemradio`
 *
 * @event change - (React: onChange) This event is dispatched when a `menuitemcheckbox`, or `menuitemradio` changes.
 * @event action - (React: onAction) This event is dispatched when a `menuitem` selected.
 */

declarations.description:
`mdc-navmenuitem` is a menuitem styled to work as a navigation tab.
It supports a leading icon, optional badge and dynamic text rendering.

Note: `mdc-navmenuitem` is intended to be used inside `mdc-menubar` as part of the sideNavigation
component. Its structure, spacing, and interactions are designed to align with
the visual and functional requirements of side navigation layouts.
declarations.name: NavMenuItem

members.name: active
members.description:
The navmenuitem's active state indicates whether it is currently toggled on (active) or off (inactive).
When the active state is true, the navmenuitem is considered to be in an active state, meaning it is toggled on.
Conversely, when the active state is false, the navmenuitem is in an inactive state, indicating it is toggled off.

members.name: badgeType
members.description:
Type of the badge
Can be `dot` (notification) or `counter`.

members.name: counter
members.description: Counter is the number which can be provided in the badge.

members.name: maxCounter
members.description:
The maximum number can be set up to 999, anything above that will be rendered as _999+_.
The max counter can be `9`, `99` or `999`.

members.name: navId
members.description:
Id of the navMenuItem (used as a identificator when used in the menubar)
Note: It has to be unique.

members.name: ariaLabel
members.description: Aria-label attribute to be set for accessibility

members.name: disableAriaCurrent
members.description:
When set to true, prevents the automatic setting of the `aria-current` attribute on the navmenuitem
when it becomes active. This is useful for cases where you want to maintain the visual active styling
but need to handle aria-current attribute differently or not at all.
The active button styling will still be applied regardless of this setting.

members.name: isNested
members.description:
Check whether the navmenuitem is inside a nested nav structure.
Returns `true` if there is a parent `mdc-menupopover`
This method assumes nesting implies deeper levels of nav hierarchy.

members.name: iconName
members.description:
Name of the icon (= filename).

If no `icon-name` is provided, no icon will be rendered.

members.name: arrowPosition
members.description:
Defines where the arrow icon will appear.
- `'leading'`: Icon appears on the leading edge (start).
- `'trailing'`: Icon appears on the trailing edge (end).

If not set, no arrow is displayed.

members.name: arrowDirection
members.description:
Defines the direction the arrow icon points.
- `'positive'`: Arrow points toward the trailing side.
- `'negative'`: Arrow points toward the leading side.

members.name: name
members.description: The name attribute is used to identify the menu item when it is selected.

members.name: value
members.description:
The value attribute is used to represent a value when the menu item is selected.
It is typically used with checkbox and radio menu items, but can be handy for any menu item.

members.name: handleKeyDown

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: The keyboard event that triggered the action.

members.description:
Handles the keydown event for the menu item.
If the Enter key is pressed, it triggers a click event on the menu item.
This allows keyboard users to activate the menu item using the Enter key.

This follows the native behaviour, actionable element can be triggered by Enter
key on the keydown event.

Note: Action triggered by Space on the keyup event.

members.name: handleKeyUp

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: The keyboard event that triggered the action.

members.description:
Handles the keyup event for the menu item.
If the Space key is released, it triggers a click event on the menu item.
This allows keyboard users to activate the menu item using the Space key.
It also prevents the default action of the Space key to avoid scrolling the page.

This follows the native behaviour, actionable element can be triggered by Space
key on the keyup event.

Note: Action triggered by Enter on the keydown event.

members.name: renderTrailingControls
members.description:
Renders the trailing controls slot and optionally the trailing arrow icon,
based on `arrowPosition` and `arrowDirection`.

members.name: renderLeadingControls
members.description:
Renders the leading controls slot and optionally the leading arrow icon,
based on `arrowPosition` and `arrowDirection`.

members.name: disabled
members.description:
Indicates whether the component is disabled.
When the component is disabled for user interaction; it is not focusable or clickable.

members.name: tabIndex
members.description: This property specifies the tab order of the element.

members.name: variant
members.description: The variant of the list item. It can be a pill, rectangle or a full-width.

members.name: label
members.description:
The primary label of the list item.
This appears on the leading side of the list item.

members.name: secondaryLabel
members.description:
The secondary label of the list item.
This appears on the leading side of the list item.

members.name: tertiaryLabel
members.description:
The tertiary label of the list item.
This appears on the leading side of the list item.

members.name: sideHeaderText
members.description:
The header text of the list item.
This appears on the trailing side of the list item.

members.name: sublineText
members.description:
The subline text of the list item.
This appears on the trailing side of the list item.

members.name: softDisabled
members.description:
Indicates whether the element is soft disabled.
When set to `true`, the element appears visually disabled but still allows
focus, click, and keyboard actions to be passed through.

**Important:** When using soft disabled, consumers must ensure that
the element behaves like a disabled element, allowing only focus and
preventing any interactions (clicks or keyboard actions) from triggering unintended actions.

members.name: tooltipText
members.description: The tooltip text is displayed on hover of the list item.

members.name: tooltipPlacement
members.description:
The tooltip placement of the list item. If the tooltip text is present,
then this tooltip placement will be applied.

members.description: Triggers a click event on the list item.

members.name: handleClick
members.description:
Handles the click event on the list item.
If the tooltip is open, it has to be closed first.

members.name: displayTooltipForLongText
members.description:
Display a tooltip for the listitem.
Create the tooltip programmatically after the nearest parent element.

members.name: hideTooltipOnLeave
members.description:
Removes the dynamically created tooltip for long text label on focus or mouse leave.
This is triggered on focusout and mouseout events.

attributes.name: active
attributes.description:
The navmenuitem's active state indicates whether it is currently toggled on (active) or off (inactive).
When the active state is true, the navmenuitem is considered to be in an active state, meaning it is toggled on.
Conversely, when the active state is false, the navmenuitem is in an inactive state, indicating it is toggled off.

attributes.name: badge-type
attributes.description:
Type of the badge
Can be `dot` (notification) or `counter`.

attributes.name: counter
attributes.description: Counter is the number which can be provided in the badge.

attributes.name: max-counter
attributes.description:
The maximum number can be set up to 999, anything above that will be rendered as _999+_.
The max counter can be `9`, `99` or `999`.

attributes.name: nav-id
attributes.description:
Id of the navMenuItem (used as a identificator when used in the menubar)
Note: It has to be unique.

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description: Aria-label attribute to be set for accessibility

attributes.name: disable-aria-current
attributes.description:
When set to true, prevents the automatic setting of the `aria-current` attribute on the navmenuitem
when it becomes active. This is useful for cases where you want to maintain the visual active styling
but need to handle aria-current attribute differently or not at all.
The active button styling will still be applied regardless of this setting.

attributes.name: icon-name
attributes.description:
Name of the icon (= filename).

If no `icon-name` is provided, no icon will be rendered.

attributes.name: arrow-position
attributes.description:
Defines where the arrow icon will appear.
- `'leading'`: Icon appears on the leading edge (start).
- `'trailing'`: Icon appears on the trailing edge (end).

If not set, no arrow is displayed.

attributes.name: arrow-direction
attributes.description:
Defines the direction the arrow icon points.
- `'positive'`: Arrow points toward the trailing side.
- `'negative'`: Arrow points toward the leading side.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name attribute is used to identify the menu item when it is selected.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
The value attribute is used to represent a value when the menu item is selected.
It is typically used with checkbox and radio menu items, but can be handy for any menu item.

declarations.jsDoc:
/**
 * `mdc-navmenuitem` is a menuitem styled to work as a navigation tab.
 * It supports a leading icon, optional badge and dynamic text rendering.
 *
 * Note: `mdc-navmenuitem` is intended to be used inside `mdc-menubar` as part of the sideNavigation
 * component. Its structure, spacing, and interactions are designed to align with
 * the visual and functional requirements of side navigation layouts.
 *
 * @tagname mdc-navmenuitem
 *
 * @dependency mdc-icon
 * @dependency mdc-text
 * @dependency mdc-badge
 * @dependency mdc-tooltip
 *
 * @event click - (React: onClick) This event is dispatched when the navmenuitem is clicked.
 * @event keydown - (React: onKeyDown) This event is dispatched when a key is pressed down on the navmenuitem.
 * @event keyup - (React: onKeyUp) This event is dispatched when a key is released on the navmenuitem.
 * @event focus - (React: onFocus) This event is dispatched when the navmenuitem receives focus.
 * @event activechange - (React: onActiveChange) Dispatched when the active state of the navmenuitem changes.
 *
 * @cssproperty --mdc-navmenuitem-color - Text color of the navmenuitem in its normal state.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-navmenuitem-border-color - Border color of the navmenuitem in its normal state.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-navmenuitem-disabled-color - Text color of the navmenuitem when disabled.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-navmenuitem-expanded-width - Width of the navmenuitem when expanded.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-navmenuitem-hover-background-color - Background color of the navmenuitem when hovered.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-navmenuitem-hover-active-background-color - Background color of the active navmenuitem when hovered.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-navmenuitem-pressed-background-color - Background color of the navmenuitem when pressed.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-navmenuitem-pressed-active-background-color - Background color of the active navmenuitem when pressed.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-navmenuitem-disabled-background-color - Background color of the navmenuitem when disabled.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-navmenuitem-disabled-active-background-color - Background color of the active navmenuitem when disabled.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-navmenuitem-rest-active-background-color - Background color of the active nav item in its rest state.
 */

declarations.description:
Option component, which is used within Selectlistbox within Select component.

The label and secondary label of the option can be set using the `label` and `secondaryLabel` properties respectively.
The `label` is the primary text displayed in the option, while the `secondaryLabel` is the secondary text displayed below the primary label.

The `selected` property is used to indicate whether the option is selected or not.
When the `selected` property is set to true, a checkmark icon will be displayed
on the right side of the option to indicate that it is selected.

The `prefixIcon` property can be used to display an icon on the left side of the option label.
We can show a tooltip by setting `tooltip-text` attribute. This will be displayed on hover or focus of the option.
The tooltip will be helpful for a long label text which is truncated with ellipsis.

members.name: validationMessage
members.description:
Custom validation message that will override the default message and displayed when the input is invalid.

To display custom validation messages, you must listen for input events (or other relevant events)
on your component and update the `validationMessage` property with the desired message string.
Updating this property will ensure that new validation messages are shown to the user.
- The `validationMessage` property overrides the default browser validation message when set.
- Consumers are responsible for updating `validationMessage` in response to input or validation state changes.

declarations.description:
`mdc-password` is a component that allows users to input their password.
 It extends the `mdc-input` component and provides additional features specific to password fields.
 It contains:
- `label` field - describe the password field.
- `password` field - contains the value
- `help-text` or `validation-message` - displayed below the password field.
- `help-text-type` - type of the help text, can be 'default', 'error', 'warning', 'success', 'priority'.
- `show-hide-button-aria-label` - aria label for the trailing show/hide button.
- all the attributes of the native password field.

The Aria-Label attribute to be set for accessibility.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: The placeholder text that is displayed when the input field is empty.

members.name: readonly
members.description: readonly attribute of the input field. If true, the input field is read-only.

members.name: prefixText
members.description:
The prefix text that is displayed before the input field. It has a max length of 10 characters.
When the prefix text is set, make sure to set the 'data-aria-label'
attribute with the appropriate value for accessibility.

members.name: leadingIcon
members.description: The leading icon that is displayed before the input field.

members.name: trailingButton
members.description: The trailing button when set to true, shows a clear button that clears the input field.

members.name: maxlength
members.description: The maximum number of characters that the input field can accept.

members.name: minlength
members.description: The minimum number of characters that the input field can accept.

members.name: autocapitalize
members.description: The autocapitalize attribute of the input field.

members.name: autocomplete
members.description: The autocomplete attribute of the input field.

members.name: autofocus
members.description: If true, the input field is focused when the component is rendered.

members.name: dirname
members.description: Specifies the name of the directionality of text for submission purposes (e.g., "rtl" for right-to-left).

members.name: pattern
members.description:
The pattern attribute of the input field.
Specifies a regular expression that the input value must match for validation purposes.

members.name: list
members.description:
The list attribute of the input field.
Identifies a list of pre-defined options to suggest to the user.

members.name: size
members.description:
The size attribute of the input field.
Specifies the width of the input field.

members.name: clearAriaLabel
members.description: Aria label for the trailing button. If trailing button is set to true, this label is used for the clear button.

members.name: updateValue
members.description:
Updates the value of the input field.
Sets the form value.

members.name: onInput
members.description:
Handles the input event of the input field.
Updates the value and sets the validity of the input field.

members.name: onChange

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: Event which contains information about the value change.

members.description:
Handles the change event of the input field.
Updates the value and sets the validity of the input field.

The 'change' event does not bubble up through the shadow DOM as it was not composed.
Therefore, we need to re-dispatch the same event to ensure it is propagated correctly.
Read more: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Event/composed

members.name: handleKeyDown
members.description:
Handles the keydown event of the input field.
If the key pressed is 'Enter', it submits the form.

members.name: renderLeadingIcon
members.description:
Renders the leading icon before the input field.
If the leading icon is not set, it will not be displayed.

members.name: renderPrefixText
members.description:
Renders the prefix text before the input field.
If the prefix text is more than 10 characters,
- it will not be displayed.
- the validation messsage will be displayed.

 Note: We are setting aria-hidden so that the screen reader does not read the prefix text.
 The consumers should set the appropriate aria-label for the input field using 'data-aria-label' attribute.

members.name: clearInputText
members.description: Clears the input field.

members.name: disabled
members.description:
Indicates whether the component is disabled.
When the component is disabled for user interaction; it is not focusable or clickable.

members.name: label
members.description: The label of the input field. It is linked to the input field using the `for` attribute.

members.name: required
members.description:
The required attribute to indicate that the input field is required.
It is used to append a required indicator (*) to the label.

members.name: helpText
members.description: The help text that is displayed below the input field.

members.name: toggletipText
members.description:
The toggletip text that is displayed when the label is hovered.
It is used to provide additional information about the label.

members.name: toggletipPlacement
members.description: The placement of the toggletip that is displayed when the info icon is hovered.

members.name: infoIconAriaLabel
members.description:
Aria label for the info icon that is displayed next to the label when `toggletipText` is set.
This is used for accessibility purposes to provide a description of the icon.

members.name: renderLabelElement
members.description:
creates the label element when the label property is set.
id is used to link the label with the input field.

members.name: renderHelpTextIcon
members.description:
creates the helpertext icon based on the helpTextType for validation.
If the helpTextType is not set, it defaults to 'default' and it doesn't display any icon.

members.name: renderHelpText
members.description:
creates the helper text component when the helpertext value is set.
It is also used to display the validation message based on the helpTextType.

members.name: renderLabel
members.description: renders the mdc-label-text container that contains the label and labelInfoToggleTip.

members.name: renderHelperText
members.description: renders the mdc-help-text container that contains the helpertext icon and helpertext.

attributes.name: validation-message
attributes.description:
Custom validation message that will override the default message and displayed when the input is invalid.

To display custom validation messages, you must listen for input events (or other relevant events)
on your component and update the `validationMessage` property with the desired message string.
Updating this property will ensure that new validation messages are shown to the user.
- The `validationMessage` property overrides the default browser validation message when set.
- Consumers are responsible for updating `validationMessage` in response to input or validation state changes.

declarations.jsDoc:
/**
 * `mdc-password` is a component that allows users to input their password.
 *  It extends the `mdc-input` component and provides additional features specific to password fields.
 *  It contains:
 * - `label` field - describe the password field.
 * - `password` field - contains the value
 * - `help-text` or `validation-message` - displayed below the password field.
 * - `help-text-type` - type of the help text, can be 'default', 'error', 'warning', 'success', 'priority'.
 * - `show-hide-button-aria-label` - aria label for the trailing show/hide button.
 * - all the attributes of the native password field.
 *
 * @tagname mdc-password
 *
 * @event input - (React: onInput) This event is dispatched when the value of the password field changes (every press).
 * @event change - (React: onChange) This event is dispatched when the value of the password field changes (on blur).
 * @event focus - (React: onFocus) This event is dispatched when the password receives focus.
 * @event blur - (React: onBlur) This event is dispatched when the password loses focus.
 *
 * @dependency mdc-icon
 * @dependency mdc-text
 * @dependency mdc-button
 * @dependency mdc-toggletip
 *
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-disabled-border-color - Border color for the password container when disabled
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-disabled-text-color - Text color for the password field when disabled
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-disabled-background-color - Background color for the password field when disabled
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-border-color - Border color for the password container
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-text-color - Text color for the password field
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-background-color - Background color for the password field
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-selection-background-color - Background color for the selected text
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-selection-text-color - Text color for the selected text
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-support-text-color - Text color for the help text
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-hover-background-color - Background color for the password field when hovered
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-focused-background-color - Background color for the password field when focused
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-focused-border-color - Border color for the password container when focused
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-error-border-color - Border color for the password container when error
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-warning-border-color - Border color for the password container when warning
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-success-border-color - Border color for the password container when success
 * @cssproperty --mdc-input-primary-border-color - Border color for the password container when primary
 *
 */

declarations.description:
Popover component is a lightweight floating UI element that displays additional content when triggered.
It can be used for tooltips, dropdowns, or contextual menus.
The popover automatically positions itself based on available space and
supports dynamic height adjustments with scrollable content when needed。
declarations.name: Popover

cssProperties.description: border color of the popover
cssProperties.name: --mdc-popover-border-color

cssProperties.description: inverted background color of the popover
cssProperties.name: --mdc-popover-inverted-background-color

cssProperties.description: inverted border color of the popover
cssProperties.name: --mdc-popover-inverted-border-color

cssProperties.description: inverted text color of the popover
cssProperties.name: --mdc-popover-inverted-text-color

cssProperties.description: elevation of the popover

slots.description: Default slot for the popover content

members.name: id
members.description: The unique ID of the popover.

members.name: triggerID
members.description:
The ID of the element that triggers the popover.
This attribute is required for the popover to work.

members.name: trigger
members.description:
Determines the events that cause the Popover to show.
Multiple event names should be separated by spaces.
For example to allow both click and hover, use 'click mouseenter' as the trigger.
- **click**
- **mouseenter**
- **focusin**
- **manual**

members.name: placement
members.description:
The placement of the popover.
- **top**
- **top-start**
- **top-end**
- **bottom**
- **bottom-start**
- **bottom-end**
- **left**
- **left-start**
- **left-end**
- **right**
- **right-start**
- **right-end**

members.name: color
members.description:
Color of the popover
- **tonal**
- **contrast**

members.name: visible
members.description: The visibility of the popover.

members.name: offset
members.description: The offset of the popover.

members.name: boundary
members.description:
This describes the clipping element(s) or area that overflow will be checked relative to.
The default is 'clippingAncestors', which are the overflow ancestors which will cause the
element to be clipped.

Possible values:
 - 'clippingAncestors'
 - any css selector

members.name: boundaryRoot
members.description:
This describes the root boundary that the element will be checked for overflow relative to.
The default is 'viewport', which is the area of the page the user can see on the screen.

The other string option is 'document', which is the entire page outside the viewport.

members.name: boundaryPadding
members.description:
Virtual padding around the boundary to check for overflow.
So the popover will not be placed on top of the edge of the boundary.

Default works well for most cases, but you can set this to customise it when necessary.

members.name: focusTrap
members.description:
Determines whether the focus trap is enabled.
If true, focus will be restricted to the content within this component.

members.name: preventScroll
members.description: Prevent outside scrolling when popover is shown.

members.name: showArrow
members.description: The arrow visibility of the popover.

members.name: closeButton
members.description: The close button visibility of the popover.

members.name: interactive
members.description:
Determines whether the popover is interactive.
Make sure to set focusTrap to true to keep the focus inside the popover in case necessary.
Setting interactive to true will not automatically set focusTrap!

members.name: delay
members.description: The delay of the show/hide popover.

members.name: hideOnEscape
members.description: Hide popover on escape key press.

members.name: propagateEventOnEscape
members.description:
Propagates the event, when the escape key is pressed (only when pressed inside the popover)
If true, the escape key press close the popover and will propagate the keydown event.
If false, the escape key press will close the popover but will not propagate the keydown event.
(set to false to prevent the event from bubbling up to the document).

This only works when `hideOnEscape` is true.

members.name: hideOnBlur
members.description: Hide popover on blur.

members.name: hideOnOutsideClick
members.description: Hide on outside click of the popover.

members.name: focusBackToTrigger
members.description: The focus back to trigger after the popover hide.

members.name: backdrop
members.description:
Determines whether the popover with backdrop.
Other than popover and trigger element, the rest of the screen will be covered with a backdrop.

members.name: backdropAppendTo
members.description: Element ID the backdrop will be a append to (if `backdrop` is true).

members.name: isBackdropInvisible
members.description:
Set this property to false to make the backdrop a dimmed visible backdrop (if `backdrop` is true).
This is useful for cases where the popovers backdrop should get dimmed.
members.default: true - default is to have a invisible backdrop

members.name: flip
members.description: Changes the placement of popover to keep it in view when scrolling.

members.name: size
members.description: Changes the size of popover to keep it in view when scrolling.

members.name: zIndex
members.description: The z-index of the popover.

members.name: appendTo
members.description: Element ID that the popover append to.

members.name: closeButtonAriaLabel
members.description: aria-label attribute to be set for close button accessibility.

members.name: strategy
members.description:
The strategy of the popover.
This determines how the popover is positioned in the DOM.
- **absolute**: The popover is positioned absolutely relative to the nearest positioned ancestor.
- **fixed**: The popover is positioned fixed relative to the viewport.

Default as `absolute` is recommended for most cases.
In cases where the popover gets clipped by a scrollable container,
you can set this to `fixed` to avoid clipping.

members.name: role
members.description: Role of the popover

members.name: ariaLabelledby
members.description:
aria-labelledby for an interactive popover only, defaults to the trigger component id.
Used in nested cases where the triggerComponent isn't the actual button.

members.name: ariaDescribedby
members.description: aria-describedby of the popover.

members.name: disableAriaExpanded
members.description:
Disable setting the aria-expanded attribute on trigger element.
Make sure to set this to false when the popover is interactive.

members.name: disableAriaHasPopup
members.description:
Disable setting the aria-haspopup attribute on trigger element.
Make sure to set this to true when the popover is extended and its role
is not 'dialog' or 'alertdialog' i.e. listbox, menu, etc.

members.name: keepConnectedTooltipClosed
members.description:
If a tooltip is connected to the same trigger element,
this property will keep the connected tooltip closed if this popover is open.
This is useful when you want to show a popover with a tooltip
but you don't want the tooltip to be shown at the same time.

members.name: setupTriggerListeners
members.description:
Sets up the trigger related event listeners, based on the trigger type.
Includes fallback for mouseenter trigger to also handle focusin for non-interactive popovers.

members.name: removeTriggerListeners
members.description: Removes the trigger related event listeners.

members.name: removeAllListeners
members.description: Removes all event listeners related to the popover.

members.name: onOutsidePopoverClick
members.description: Handles the outside click event to close the popover.

members.name: onEscapeKeydown
members.description:
Handles the escape keydown event to close the popover.

This method is attached to the document.

members.name: onPopoverFocusOut
members.description: Handles the popover focus out event.

members.name: isOpenUpdated

parameters.name: oldValue
parameters.description: The old value of the visible property.

parameters.name: newValue
parameters.description: The new value of the visible property.

members.description:
Handles the popover visibility change and position the popover.
Handles the exit event to close the popover.

members.name: handleMouseEnter
members.description:
Handles mouse enter event on the trigger element.
This method sets the `isHovered` flag to true and shows the popover

members.name: handleMouseLeave
members.description:
Handles mouse leave event on the trigger element.
This method sets the `isHovered` flag to false and starts the close delay
timer to hide the popover.

members.name: handleFocusOut
members.description:
Handles focus out event on the trigger element.
This method checks if the popover is not hovered and hides the popover.
If the popover is hovered, it will not hide the popover.

members.name: handleFocusIn
members.description:
Handles focus in event on the trigger element.
This method checks if the trigger element has visible focus or is being hovered.

members.name: startCloseDelay
members.description:
Starts the close delay timer.
If the popover is not interactive, it will close the popover after the delay.

members.name: cancelCloseDelay
members.description: Cancels the close delay timer.

members.name: show
members.description: Shows the popover.

members.name: hide
members.description: Hides the popover.

members.name: togglePopoverVisible
members.description: Toggles the popover visibility.

members.name: positionPopover
members.description:
Positions the popover based on the trigger element.
It also handles the flip, size and arrow placement.
It uses the floating-ui/dom library to calculate the position.

members.name: findClosestPopover
members.description:
Finds the closest popover to the passed element in the DOM tree.

Useful when need to find the parent popover in a nested popover scenario.

parameters.description: The element to start searching from.
parameters.name: element

members.name: shouldFocusTrapWrap
members.description:
Determines whether focus should wrap around when reaching the first or last focusable element.
If true, focus will cycle from end to start and vice versa.

This only applies when `focusTrap` is true.

members.name: activateFocusTrap
members.description: Activate the focus trap

members.name: deactivateFocusTrap
members.description: Deactivate the focus trap.

members.name: hasNoClientRects
members.description: Checks if the element has no client rectangles (not visible in the viewport).

members.name: hasZeroDimensions
members.description: Checks if the element has zero dimensions (width and height are both 0).

members.name: isNotVisible
members.description: Determines if the element is not visible in the DOM.

members.name: hasHiddenStyle
members.description: Checks if the element has inline styles that make it hidden.

members.name: hasComputedHidden
members.description: Checks if the element is hidden by a computed style.

members.name: isHidden
members.description: Checks if the element is hidden from the user.

members.name: isDisabled
members.description: Checks if the element is disabled.

members.name: isNotTabbable
members.description: Checks if the element is not tabbable.

members.name: isInteractiveElement
members.description: Checks if the element is interactive.

members.name: isFocusable
members.description: Checks if the element is focusable.

members.name: findFocusable

parameters.name: root
parameters.description: The root element to search for focusable elements.

members.description:
Recursively finds all focusable elements within the given root and its descendants.

Make sure this is performant, as it will be called multiple times.

members.name: setFocusableElements
members.description: Updates the list of focusable elements within the component's shadow root.

members.name: setInitialFocus

parameters.name: elementIndexToReceiveFocus
parameters.description: The index of the preferable element to focus.

members.description: Sets the initial focus within the container.

members.name: calculateNextIndex

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: The step to calculate the next index.

members.description: Calculates the next index for the focus trap.

members.name: getDeepActiveElement
members.description: Returns the deepest active element in the shadow DOM.

members.name: findElement
members.description: Finds the index of the active element within the focusable elements.

members.name: isEqualFocusNode
members.description: Checks if the active element is equal to the given element.

members.name: trapFocus

parameters.description:
The direction of the focus trap.
If true, the focus will be trapped in the previous element.
parameters.name: direction

members.description: Traps focus within the container.

members.name: handleTabKeydown
members.description: Traps focus within the container.

events.description: (React: onDestroyed) This event is dispatched when the popover is destroyed (removed from the DOM)

attributes.description: Hide popover on blur.

attributes.name: hide-on-outside-click
attributes.description: Hide on outside click of the popover.

attributes.name: focus-back-to-trigger
attributes.description: The focus back to trigger after the popover hide.

attributes.name: backdrop
attributes.description:
Determines whether the popover with backdrop.
Other than popover and trigger element, the rest of the screen will be covered with a backdrop.

attributes.name: backdrop-append-to
attributes.description: Element ID the backdrop will be a append to (if `backdrop` is true).

attributes.name: is-backdrop-invisible
attributes.description:
Set this property to false to make the backdrop a dimmed visible backdrop (if `backdrop` is true).
This is useful for cases where the popovers backdrop should get dimmed.
attributes.default: true - default is to have a invisible backdrop

attributes.name: flip
attributes.description: Changes the placement of popover to keep it in view when scrolling.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: Changes the size of popover to keep it in view when scrolling.

attributes.name: z-index
attributes.description: The z-index of the popover.

attributes.name: append-to
attributes.description: Element ID that the popover append to.

attributes.name: close-button-aria-label
attributes.description: aria-label attribute to be set for close button accessibility.

attributes.name: strategy
attributes.description:
The strategy of the popover.
This determines how the popover is positioned in the DOM.
- **absolute**: The popover is positioned absolutely relative to the nearest positioned ancestor.
- **fixed**: The popover is positioned fixed relative to the viewport.

Default as `absolute` is recommended for most cases.
In cases where the popover gets clipped by a scrollable container,
you can set this to `fixed` to avoid clipping.

attributes.name: role
attributes.description: Role of the popover

attributes.name: aria-labelledby
attributes.description:
aria-labelledby for an interactive popover only, defaults to the trigger component id.
Used in nested cases where the triggerComponent isn't the actual button.

attributes.name: aria-describedby
attributes.description: aria-describedby of the popover.

attributes.name: disable-aria-expanded
attributes.description:
Disable setting the aria-expanded attribute on trigger element.
Make sure to set this to false when the popover is interactive.

attributes.name: disable-aria-haspopup
attributes.description:
Disable setting the aria-haspopup attribute on trigger element.
Make sure to set this to true when the popover is extended and its role
is not 'dialog' or 'alertdialog' i.e. listbox, menu, etc.

attributes.name: keep-connected-tooltip-closed
attributes.description:
If a tooltip is connected to the same trigger element,
this property will keep the connected tooltip closed if this popover is open.
This is useful when you want to show a popover with a tooltip
but you don't want the tooltip to be shown at the same time.

attributes.name: should-focus-trap-wrap
attributes.description:
Determines whether focus should wrap around when reaching the first or last focusable element.
If true, focus will cycle from end to start and vice versa.

This only applies when `focusTrap` is true.

declarations.jsDoc:
/**
 * Popover component is a lightweight floating UI element that displays additional content when triggered.
 * It can be used for tooltips, dropdowns, or contextual menus.
 * The popover automatically positions itself based on available space and
 * supports dynamic height adjustments with scrollable content when needed。
 *
 * @dependency mdc-button
 *
 * @tagname mdc-popover
 *
 *
 * @event shown - (React: onShown) This event is dispatched when the popover is shown
 * @event hidden - (React: onHidden) This event is dispatched when the popover is hidden
 * @event created - (React: onCreated) This event is dispatched when the popover is created (added to the DOM)
 * @event destroyed - (React: onDestroyed) This event is dispatched when the popover is destroyed (removed from the DOM)
 *
 * @cssproperty --mdc-popover-arrow-border-radius - radius of the arrow border
 * @cssproperty --mdc-popover-arrow-border - border of the arrow
 * @cssproperty --mdc-popover-primary-background-color - primary background color of the popover
 * @cssproperty --mdc-popover-border-color - border color of the popover
 * @cssproperty --mdc-popover-inverted-background-color - inverted background color of the popover
 * @cssproperty --mdc-popover-inverted-border-color - inverted border color of the popover
 * @cssproperty --mdc-popover-inverted-text-color - inverted text color of the popover
 * @cssproperty --mdc-popover-elevation-3 - elevation of the popover
 * @cssproperty --mdc-popover-max-width - max width of the popover
 * @cssproperty --mdc-popover-max-height - max height of the popover
 *
 * @slot - Default slot for the popover content
 *
 */

declarations.description:
mdc-progressbar component visually represents a progress indicator, typically used to show
the completion state of an ongoing process (e.g., loading, file upload, etc.).
It contains an optional label and an optional helper text.

- It supports mainly two types: Default and Inline
- It supports three validation variants: Default, Success and Error.

This component is created by extending FormfieldWrapper.

When the component is disabled for user interaction; it is not focusable or clickable.

members.name: label
members.description: The label of the input field. It is linked to the input field using the `for` attribute.

members.name: required
members.description:
The required attribute to indicate that the input field is required.
It is used to append a required indicator (*) to the label.

members.name: helpTextType
members.description: The type of help text. It can be 'default', 'error', 'warning', 'success', 'priority'.

members.name: helpText
members.description: The help text that is displayed below the input field.

members.name: toggletipText
members.description:
The toggletip text that is displayed when the label is hovered.
It is used to provide additional information about the label.

members.description: The placement of the toggletip that is displayed when the info icon is hovered.

members.name: infoIconAriaLabel
members.description:
Aria label for the info icon that is displayed next to the label when `toggletipText` is set.
This is used for accessibility purposes to provide a description of the icon.

members.name: renderLabelElement
members.description:
creates the label element when the label property is set.
id is used to link the label with the input field.

members.name: renderHelpTextIcon
members.description:
creates the helpertext icon based on the helpTextType for validation.
If the helpTextType is not set, it defaults to 'default' and it doesn't display any icon.

members.name: renderHelpText
members.description:
creates the helper text component when the helpertext value is set.
It is also used to display the validation message based on the helpTextType.

members.name: renderLabel
members.description: renders the mdc-label-text container that contains the label and labelInfoToggleTip.

members.name: renderHelperText

/**
 * mdc-progressbar component visually represents a progress indicator, typically used to show
 * the completion state of an ongoing process (e.g., loading, file upload, etc.).
 * It contains an optional label and an optional helper text.
 *
 * - It supports mainly two types: Default and Inline
 * - It supports three validation variants: Default, Success and Error.

 *
 * @tagname mdc-progressbar
 *
 * @dependency mdc-icon

 *
 * @cssproperty --mdc-progressbar-default-background-color - Background color of the progressbar when inactive.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-progressbar-default-active-background-color - Background color of the progressbar when active.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-progressbar-success-background-color - Background color of the progressbar when in success state.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-progressbar-error-background-color - Background color of the progressbar when in error state.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-progressbar-height - The height of the progressbar.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-progressbar-border-radius - The border radius of the progressbar.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-progressbar-label-color - Color of the progressbar label text.

 * @cssproperty --mdc-progressbar-label-fontsize - Font size of the label text.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-progressbar-label-fontweight - Font weight of the label text.
 * @cssproperty --mdc-progressbar-help-text-color - Color of the help text.
 */

declarations.description:
`mdc-progressspinner` is a customizable, circular progress indicator component.
It visually represents the current completion state of a process, such as loading,
syncing, uploading, or any ongoing task that has a measurable percentage.

The spinner is built using SVG with two concentric `<circle>` elements:
- The `progress` arc represents the portion of work completed.
- The `track` arc represents the remaining part.

A visual gap is maintained between the progress and track arcs to clearly
distinguish the two segments. The component smoothly animates arc length
and respects accessibility best practices with ARIA attributes.

The component supports different states:
- **Default**: Circular spinner shows the progress.
- **Success**: Displays a checkmark icon when progress reaches 100%.
- **Error**: Displays an error icon when in an error state.

members.name: updateRadio

parameters.name: enabledRadios

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the radio button to be updated within the enabled radios array.

members.description:
Updates the state of the radio button at the specified index within the enabled radios.
Focuses the radio button and triggers the change event if the radio button is not read-only.

members.name: handleKeyDown
members.description: Handles the keydown event (Arrow Up/Down/Left/Right) on the radio element.

members.name: updateTabIndex
members.description:
Update tab index for all radios in the same group (name)
If any radio group is checked, it will have a tab index of 0
If no radio group is checked, the first enabled radio will have a tab index of 0

members.name: validationMessage
members.description:
Custom validation message that will override the default message and displayed when the input is invalid.

To display custom validation messages, you must listen for input events (or other relevant events)
on your component and update the `validationMessage` property with the desired message string.
Updating this property will ensure that new validation messages are shown to the user.
- The `validationMessage` property overrides the default browser validation message when set.
- Consumers are responsible for updating `validationMessage` in response to input or validation state changes.

declarations.jsDoc:
/**
 * Radio allow users to select single options from a list or turn an item/feature on or off.
 * These are often used in forms, settings, and selection in lists.
 *
 * A radio component contains an optional label, optional info icon and an optional helper text.
 *
 * @dependency mdc-button
 * @dependency mdc-icon
 * @dependency mdc-text
 * @dependency mdc-staticradio
 * @dependency mdc-toggletip
 *
 * @tagname mdc-radio
 *
 * @event change - (React: onChange) Event that gets dispatched when the radio state changes.
 * @event focus - (React: onFocus) Event that gets dispatched when the radio receives focus.
 *
 * @cssproperty --mdc-radio-text-disabled-color - color of the label when disabled
 * @cssproperty --mdc-radio-control-inactive-hover - color of the radio button when inactive and hovered
 * @cssproperty --mdc-radio-control-inactive-pressed-color - color of the radio button when inactive and pressed
 * @cssproperty --mdc-radio-control-active-hover-color - color of the radio button when active and hovered
 * @cssproperty --mdc-radio-control-active-pressed-color - color of the radio button when active and pressed
 * @cssproperty --mdc-radio-disabled-border-color - color of the radio button when disabled
 * @cssproperty --mdc-radio-control-active-disabled-background - color of the radio button when active and disabled
 * @cssproperty --mdc-radio-control-inactive-disabled-background - color of the radio button when inactive and disabled
 *
 */

declarations.description:
`mdc-radiogroup` - This is the wrapper component for radio buttons which are grouped together.
It can have a header text and a description. It enables users to select a single option from a set of options.
It is often used in forms, settings, and selection in lists. It automatically group the radio buttons inside it.

declarations.description:
`mdc-screenreaderannouncer` can be used to announce messages with the screen reader. To make an announcement set `announcement` attribute on the `mdc-screenreaderannouncer` element. **Internal logic** When the screenreader announcer is connected to the DOM, if the `identity` attribute is not provided, it is set to `mdc-screenreaderannouncer-identity` and a `
` element with this id is created in the DOM. If the `identity` attribute is provided, the identity element is used and no new element is created in the DOM. When the `announcement` attribute is set, the screenreader announcer will create a `
` element with `aria-live` attribute set to the value of `data-aria-live` attribute and append it to the `identity` element. After delay of `delay` milliseconds, a

element with the announcement text is appended to the `

` element. The announcement `
` element is removed from the DOM after `timeout` milliseconds. When the screen announcer component is disconnected from the DOM, all the timeouts are cleared and all the announcement elements added are removed from the DOM and timeouts cleared. **Note** 1. The default delay of 150 miliseconds is used as we dynamically generate the aria-live region in the DOM and add the announcement text to it. 3. If no `identity` is provided, all the screen reader components will create and use only one `
` element with id `mdc-screenreaderannouncer-identity` in the DOM. Reference: https://patrickhlauke.github.io/aria/tests/live-regions/
declarations.name: ScreenreaderAnnouncer

members.name: announcement
members.description:
The announcement attribute is a string that is used to announce messages to the screen reader.
The announcement is made when the announcement attribute is set to a non-empty string.

members.name: identity
members.description:
The id of the element in the light dom, to which announcement elements will be appended.

If id is not provided, it will be set to `mdc-screenreaderannouncer-identity` and
a div element with this id will be created in the light dom.

members.name: dataAriaLive
members.description: Aria live value for announcement.

members.name: delay
members.description:
Milliseconds to wait before adding the announcement to the identiy region in
DOM, which will announce the message to the screen reader.

members.name: timeout
members.description:
Milliseconds to wait after which the announcement element will be removed from
identity region in DOM, causing the screen reader to not announcing the message.

members.name: announce

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: The delay in milliseconds before announcing the message.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: The timeout in milliseconds before removing the announcement.

parameters.name: ariaLive
parameters.description: The aria live value for the announcement.

members.description:
Announces the given announcement to the screen reader.

A div element with aria-live attribute set to the given ariaLive value is created
and a p element with the announcement text is appended to it.

The div element is appended to the element in the DOM identified with id as
identity attribute.

members.name: clearTimeOutsAndAnnouncements
members.description: Clears all timeouts and removes all announcements from the screen reader.

members.name: createAnnouncementAriaLiveRegion
members.description:
Creates a div element with id as identity attribute in the DOM.

If the identity attribute is not provided, it is set internally to
`mdc-screenreaderannouncer-identity`.

attributes.name: announcement
attributes.description:
The announcement attribute is a string that is used to announce messages to the screen reader.
The announcement is made when the announcement attribute is set to a non-empty string.

attributes.name: identity
attributes.description:
The id of the element in the light dom, to which announcement elements will be appended.

If id is not provided, it will be set to `mdc-screenreaderannouncer-identity` and
a div element with this id will be created in the light dom.

attributes.name: data-aria-live
attributes.description: Aria live value for announcement.

attributes.name: delay
attributes.description:
Milliseconds to wait before adding the announcement to the identiy region in
DOM, which will announce the message to the screen reader.

attributes.name: timeout
attributes.description:
Milliseconds to wait after which the announcement element will be removed from
identity region in DOM, causing the screen reader to not announcing the message.

declarations.description:
`mdc-searchfield` component is used as an input field for search functionality.

It supports `mdc-inputchip` as filters.

This component is built by extending the `mdc-input` component.
declarations.name: Searchfield

slots.description: Slot for input chips
slots.name: filters

members.name: handleKeyDown
members.description:
Handles the keydown event of the search field.
If the key pressed is 'Enter', it submits the form.
If the key pressed is 'Escape', it clears the input text.

members.name: renderInputChips
members.description:
This method is used to render the input chips inside filters slot.
It will remove any elements that are not input chips.

members.description: Clears the input field.

members.description:
Indicates the name of the component group.
They are used to group elements in a form together.

members.description:
Custom validation message that will override the default message and displayed when the input is invalid.

To display custom validation messages, you must listen for input events (or other relevant events)
on your component and update the `validationMessage` property with the desired message string.
Updating this property will ensure that new validation messages are shown to the user.

When the prefix text is set, make sure to set the 'data-aria-label'
attribute with the appropriate value for accessibility.

members.description:

members.description:

members.description:
Handles the change event of the input field.
Updates the value and sets the validity of the input field.

The 'change' event does not bubble up through the shadow DOM as it was not composed.
Therefore, we need to re-dispatch the same event to ensure it is propagated correctly.
Read more: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Event/composed

If the leading icon is not set, it will not be displayed.

members.name: renderPrefixText

If the prefix text is more than 10 characters,
- it will not be displayed.
- the validation messsage will be displayed.

 Note: We are setting aria-hidden so that the screen reader does not read the prefix text.
 The consumers should set the appropriate aria-label for the input field using 'data-aria-label' attribute.

declarations.jsDoc:
/**
 * `mdc-searchfield` component is used as an input field for search functionality.
 *
 * It supports `mdc-inputchip` as filters.
 *
 * This component is built by extending the `mdc-input` component.
 *
 * @tagname mdc-searchfield
 * 
 * @event input - (React: onInput) This event is dispatched when the value of the input field changes (every press).
 * @event change - (React: onChange) This event is dispatched when the value of the input field changes (on blur).
 * @event focus - (React: onFocus) This event is dispatched when the input receives focus.
 * @event blur - (React: onBlur) This event is dispatched when the input loses focus.
 * @event clear - (React: onClear) This event is dispatched when the input text is cleared.
 *
 * @slot filters - Slot for input chips
 */
